name: flamapy-sat

metadata.name: flamapy-sat
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)
metadata.summary: flamapy-sat is a plugin to flamapy module

documentation:
# pysat_metamodel

This repository will host the pysat metamodel and its operation implementation


## Install for development

``` bash
pip install -e .
```

## Make sure that you have installed python-dev 

``` bash
sudo apt install python-dev #python3-dev in Ubuntu derivatives
```

Default TextCNFNotation.JAVA.

documentation: Return the list of variables' names of the CNF formula.

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.CNFLogicConnective
documentation: The propositional logic connectives a formula in CNF can contain.

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFNotation
documentation:
Possible notations of a CNF formula.

Five different notations are available:
    Logical symbols:
        (A) ∧ (¬B ∨ C) ∧ ...
    Textual symbols:
        (A) and (not B or C) and ...
    Java symbols:
        (A) && (!B || C) && ...
    Java Short symbols:
        (A) & (!B | C) && ...
    Short symbols:
        (A) & (-B | C) & ...

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel.from_textual_cnf_file
documentation:
This method reads any of the available textual notations,
but only one notation at the same time,
so the .txt file should include only one of the possible notations in a single line.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel.write_textual_cnf_file
documentation:
Write the textual CNF formula as a string in a file.

Default syntax is TextCNFNotation.JAVA_SHORT: (A) & (!B | C) && ...

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel.get_textual_cnf_notation
documentation: Return the notation used for the CNF formula.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel.get_textual_cnf_formula
documentation:
Return the CNF formula in the specified notation syntax.

Default syntax is TextCNFNotation.JAVA_SHORT: (A) & (!B | C) && ...

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.models.txtcnf_model.TextCNFModel.get_variables
documentation: Return the list of variables' names in the CNF formula.

Textual representation of a conjunctive normal form (CNF) formula.

A CNF formula (or clausal normal form) is a conjunction of one or more clauses,
where a clause is a disjunction of literals.

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.transformations.cnf_to_pysat.CNFReader
documentation:
Read a CNF formula as a string representing a feature model.

The expected format is the generated by FeatureIDE when exporting the model as CNF (.txt).
That generates a file with the CNF formula in four different notations:
    Logical Symbols:
        (A) ∧ (¬B ∨ C) ∧ ...
    Textual Symbols:
        (A) and (not B or C) and ...
    Java Symbols:
        (A) && (!B || C) && ...
    Short Symbols:
        (A) & (-B | C) & ...
This class is able to read any of these notations, but only one notation,
so the .txt file should be modified to include only
one of those notations by removing the others.

functions.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.metric_method
documentation:
Decorator to mark a method as a metric method.
It has the value of the measure, it can also have a size and a ratio.
Example:
    property name: Abstract Features.
    description: The description of the property.
    value (optional): the list of abstract features.
    size (optional): the length of the list.
    ratio (optional): the percentage of abstract features with regards the total number
                        of features.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.satisfiable
documentation: A feature model is satisfiable if it represents at least one configuration.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.core_features
documentation: Features that are part of all the configurations (aka 'common features').

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.variant_features
documentation: Features that do not appear in all the configurations.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.dead_features
documentation: Features that cannot appear in any configuration.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.unique_features
documentation: Features that appear in exactly one configuration.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.false_optional_features
documentation:
Features defined as optionals the selection of their parents make the feature itself 
selected as well.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_metamodel.operations.pysat_metrics.PySATMetrics.configurations
documentation:
Number of configurations represented by the feature model. 

If <= is shown, the number represents an upper estimation bound.

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.transformations.fm_to_diag_pysat.FmToDiagPysat.add_relation
documentation: def _store_constraint_relation(self, relation: Relation, clauses: List[List[int]]) -> None:
for clause in clauses:
self.destination_model.add_clause(clause)
self.destination_model.add_clause_to_map(str(relation), clauses)

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.operations.pysat_conflict.PySATConflict
documentation:
An operation that computes conflicts and diagnoses
using the combination of HSDAG and QuickXPlain algorithms.
Four optional inputs:
- configuration - a configuration to be diagnosed
- test_case - a test case to be used for diagnosis
- max_conflicts - specify the maximum number of conflicts to be computed
- max_depth - specify the maximum depth of the HSDAG to be computed

modules.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.operations
documentation: pylint: disable=cyclic-import

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.models.pysat_diagnosis_model.DiagnosisModel

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.models.pysat_diagnosis_model.DiagnosisModel.prepare_diagnosis_task
documentation:
Execute this method after the model is built.
If a configuration is given:
    C = configuration
    B = {f0 = true} + CF (i.e., = PySATModel)
else (no configuration is given):
    a. Diagnosis the feature model
        C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
        B = {f0 = true}
    b. Diagnosis the error
        C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
        B = {f0 = true} + test_case
        where test_case is the following:
        + Dead feature: test_case = {fi = true}
        + False optional feature: test_case = {f_parent = true} & {f_child = false}

methods.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.models.pysat_diagnosis_model.DiagnosisModel.prepare_redundancy_detection_task
documentation:
This function prepares the model for WipeOutR algorithm.
Execute this method after the model is built.
C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
B = {}

This is a new version of the PySATModel class to support the following tasks:
1. Diagnosis Task
    If a configuration is given:
        C = configuration
        B = {f0 = true} + CF (i.e., = PySATModel)
    else (no configuration is given):
        a. Diagnosis the feature model
            C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
            B = {f0 = true}
        b. Diagnosis the error
            C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
            B = {f0 = true} + test_case
            where test_case is the following:
            + Dead feature: test_case = {fi = true}
            + False optional feature: test_case = {f_parent = true} & {f_child = false}
2. Redundancy Detection Task (need negative constraints)
    C = CF (i.e., = PySATModel - {f0 = true})
    B = {}

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.operations.pysat_diagnosis.PySATDiagnosis
documentation:
An operation that computes diagnoses and conflict sets
using the combination of HSDAG and FastDiag algorithms.
Four optional inputs:
- configuration - a configuration to be diagnosed
- test_case - a test case to be used for diagnosis
- max_diagnoses - specify the maximum number of diagnoses to be computed
- max_depth - specify the maximum depth of the HSDAG to be computed

classes.name: flamapy.metamodels.pysat_diagnosis_metamodel.operations.pysat_abstract_identifier.PySATAbstractIdentifier
documentation:
An abstract operation for computes conflicts or diagnoses.
Four optional inputs:
- configuration - a configuration to be diagnosed
- test_case - a test case to be used for diagnosis
- max_depth - specify the maximum depth of the HSDAG to be computed
